fact: October 2 is exactly 275 days into the year, which means there are only 90 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season and forget where half of them are!
The sun is setting about 2 minutes earlier each day around October 2, so darkness creeps up faster than you think!
October 2 falls smack in the middle of Libra season - the only zodiac sign represented by an object (scales) instead of a living thing!
Maple trees are pumping out their brightest red and orange colors right now because they're basically starving their leaves of green chlorophyll on purpose!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 2 is prime baby animal season - lambs, calves, and joeys are being born everywhere!
The moon looks extra spooky orange when it rises around this time of year because autumn air is full of dust and particles that filter out blue light!
Monarch butterflies are in the middle of their epic 3,000-mile journey to Mexico right now, and some of them are flying over your head today!
October 2 is roughly 11 days after the autumn equinox, which means nights are officially longer than days in the north!
Spiders seem to appear everywhere around this date because they've been growing all summer and are now big enough to notice - plus they're looking for warm places to hide!
The air pressure changes around early October make some people's joints ache, but it also makes sounds travel farther - that's why autumn nights seem so crisp and clear!
In northern forests, pine cones are dropping like crazy right now because trees are racing to spread their seeds before winter hits!
